name: Randy Rhoads
title: Last known public photo of Randy Rhoads, taken backstage with two fans after his final concert at Knoxville Civic Coliseum on March 18, 1982, less than twelve hours before the plane crash that killed him.
description: This is the last known public photograph of Randy Rhoads, taken backstage with two fans at the Knoxville Civic Coliseum on March 18, 1982, after his final concert with Ozzy Osbourne on the Diary of a Madman tour. The tour bus continued overnight to Leesburg, Florida, where it stopped at the home of bus driver Andrew Aycock. The next morning, March 19, 1982, Aycock took Rhoads and tour makeup artist Rachel Youngblood up in a small Beechcraft Bonanza for a joyride, repeatedly buzzing the parked tour bus. On one pass the wing clipped the bus, and the plane spun out and crashed into a nearby house, killing all three on board. Rhoads was 25 years old. Keyboardist Don Airey reportedly took photos of Rhoads on the morning of the crash, but those have never been released.
bio: Randall William Rhoads was an American heavy metal guitarist. He was a co-founding member of Quiet Riot in 1973 and, from 1979 until his death, served as lead guitarist in Ozzy Osbourne's solo band, where his neoclassical playing on Blizzard of Ozz (1980) and Diary of a Madman (1981), including the riffs to “Crazy Train” and “Mr. Crowley,” helped define the sound of 1980s heavy metal. Despite a recording career of barely three years, he is consistently ranked among the greatest guitarists of all time.

location.name: Knoxville Civic Coliseum, 500 Howard Baker Jr. Ave, Knoxville, TN 37915
